meta.question: What is APS?
meta.finalAnswer:
APS stands for **Alignment and Phasing System** in the Thirty Meter Telescope (TMT) project. 

Based on the SysML model, APS is a control system work package that includes:

- **Conceptual Design** components (APS Conceptual, APS Mission Conceptual)
- **Operational elements** (APS Mission, APS Operational Domain)
- **Black Box Specifications** (APS Operational Blackbox Specification JPL, APS Black Box Specification TMT)
- **Realization Design** (APS Realization)
- **Stakeholders** (APS User, APS Operator)
- **Interface definitions** (APS-M1CS, APS Data)

The system is organized under the Telescope's Control work packages and includes systems engineering documentation (TMT-APS DDD, TMT-APS Use Cases) and various analysis components for error budgets and alignment procedures.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?elem ?type ?name WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name . 
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?name)), LCASE("APS"))) 
  ?elem rdf:type ?type .
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Found multiple APS-related elements including blocks, packages, and documents. The paths suggest APS stands for "Alignment and Phasing System". Need to explore the main APS block/component to get a complete definition.

BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>

  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?name)), LCASE("APS")))
}
LIMIT 25

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?prop (COUNT(*) as ?count) WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name "APS Conceptual" .
  ?elem ?prop ?value .
} 
GROUP BY ?prop 
ORDER BY DESC(?count)
LIMIT 25

BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?prop (COUNT(*) as ?count) WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name "APS Conceptual" .
  ?elem ?prop ?value .
} 
GROUP BY ?prop 
ORDER BY DESC(?count)
LIMIT 25

BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?prop ?value WHERE {
  <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/TMT/block/TMT::Project::Work%2520Packages::Telescope::WorkPackages::Control::Alignment%2520and%2520Phasing%2520System::Systems%2520Engineering::01%2520Components::Operational::APS::Conceptual%2520Design::APS%2520Conceptual> ?prop ?value .
}
LIMIT 50

BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?name WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?name)), LCASE("Alignment and Phasing System")))
}
LIMIT 10

BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?elem ?name WHERE {
  ?elem rdf:type vocab:block .
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  FILTER(?name = "APS")
}
LIMIT 10

BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?commentBody WHERE {
  <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/TMT/block/TMT::Project::Work%2520Packages::Telescope::WorkPackages::Control::Alignment%2520and%2520Phasing%2520System::Systems%2520Engineering::01%2520Components::Operational::APS::Conceptual%2520Design::APS%2520Conceptual> vocab:Element_ownedComment ?comment .
  ?comment vocab:Comment_body ?commentBody .
}
LIMIT 5

BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?ownerName ?elemName WHERE {
  ?elem rdf:type vocab:block .
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?elemName .
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?elemName)), LCASE("APS")))
  ?elem vocab:Element_owner ?owner .
  OPTIONAL { ?owner vocab:NamedElement_name ?ownerName }
}
LIMIT 15

BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?qname WHERE {
  <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/TMT/block/TMT::Project::Work%2520Packages::Telescope::WorkPackages::Control::Alignment%2520and%2520Phasing%2520System::Systems%2520Engineering::01%2520Components::Operational::APS::Conceptual%2520Design::APS%2520Conceptual> vocab:NamedElement_qualifiedName ?qname .
}
LIMIT 1

request.reason: The qualified name shows APS is part of the "Alignment and Phasing System" work package. Based on the exploration, APS stands for "Alignment and Phasing System" - a control system component in the TMT (Thirty Meter Telescope) project. The model shows it has conceptual design, operational mission, black box specifications, and various subsystems.

APS stands for **Alignment and Phasing System** in the Thirty Meter Telescope (TMT) project. 

Based on the SysML model, APS is a control system work package that includes:

- **Black Box Specifications** (APS Operational Blackbox Specification JPL, APS Black Box Specification TMT)
- **Realization Design** (APS Realization)

- **Interface definitions** (APS-M1CS, APS Data)

The system is organized under the Telescope's Control work packages and includes systems engineering documentation (TMT-APS DDD, TMT-APS Use Cases) and various analysis components for error budgets and alignment procedures.
